Henrietta Lacks, born 08/01/1920 Southern Virginia, died cervical cancer
poor Tobacco farmer mother five.
silver lining behind tragic life Henrietta Lacks discovery arguably greatest scientific breakthrough medical history, first immortal cell line.
will discuss implications such discovery shortly.
first, let's delve into humbling story Henrietta Lacks legacy left behind.
greatest biological hurdles that inability cultivate cells vitro (outside living body) long enough period time, least long enough yield meaningful scientific research.
Human cells confined Hayflick limit.
That say, cells culture will divide average times before they reach cellular senescence, permanent state growth arrest.
This predicament hindered growth research countless areas science.
scientific potential powerful opportunities presented were unprecedented.
Forward, Lacks' cells were referred "HeLa Cells" (short name).
Immediately, worlds first cell production facility began producing trillion HeLa cells week.
Henrietta Lacks dying, came miracle.
October 1951 Henrietta Lacks succumbed cancer, never knowing about fortune that rightfully hers.
cells spawned unimaginable scientific oppurtunity.
decoded human chromosome, developed gene mapping, better understood bacteria, hormones, viruses (like polio), help treat diseases (like Herpes, Parkinson's, AIDS), helped treat cancel, jumpstarted cloning, genetics various forms radiation treatments.
Eleven thousand medical patents have been awarded direct consequence HeLa cells.
long till HeLa cells caught attention media.
John Hopkins questioned which patient these cells originated from.
attempted throw media providing misleading name Helene Lane.
Might say, very creative cover.
1976, after years deception lies, Rolling Stone magazine published true story.
That when Lacks' family found about Henrietta Lacks' amazing contribution.
Despite living poverty, family requested that credit should given Henrietta Lacks dying contribution mankind.
Because Henrietta Lacks death 1951, fortunate spend time with people love today.
Thank Henrietta.
